content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

content.text:
pl-1496  approved  rev 1
Name:     nightwatch patrol 2026-06-02
Seed:     seeds-6804
Template: refactor
Created:  2026-06-02T11:06:34.084Z
Updated:  2026-06-02T11:06:34.084Z
Review suggested (no reviewer recorded yet)

Sections:
  context
    Static scan of seeds@HEAD on 2026-06-02 surfaced a small cluster of quality issues that have crept in around shared CLI plumbing: an unused export, duplicated parsing logic, inconsistent error-message phrasing across commands, one command (`sd sync`) that bypasses the project's standard print/JSON-error helpers, an edge-case bug in the JSONL appenders, and stale knip configuration entries. None of these are urgent on their own, but each is a 'broken-window' that pulls the codebase away from its own conventions (CLAUDE.md: single source of truth for parsing/validation, output.ts owns user-facing prints, no dead exports, knip stays clean). Baseline gates are green: `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun test` (953 pass), `bun run check:size`, `bun run check:debt` all pass; `bunx knip` reports the unused exports and config hints fixed here.
  approach
    Land each finding as its own small PR. Order independent fixes first (dead code, knip config, error-message wording) and dependent fixes later. No public API changes, no new commands, no dependency churn. Every behavior-changing fix (appender newline normalization, sync.ts output routing) ships with a regression test that fails before the fix and passes after. The final step is the standard /release per .claude/commands/release.md, gated on every preceding step.
  steps
    1. Drop unused MIN_PRIORITY / MAX_PRIORITY exports in src/priority.ts
    2. Clean stale knip.json entries (pino-pretty, bun-types)
    3. Normalize 'Valid: a|b|c' wording across enum validation errors
    4. Replace local parsePriority in src/commands/update.ts with shared src/priority.ts helpers
    5. Route sd sync output through printSuccess + outputJson error path
    6. Fix appendIssue/appendTemplate/appendPlan trailing-newline edge case in src/store.ts
    7. Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
  acceptance
    1. bun run lint, bun run typecheck, bun test, bun run check:size, bun run check:debt, bun run check:agents all exit 0 on the final state.
    2. bunx knip reports zero unused exports and zero configuration hints.
    3. A new regression test in src/store.test.ts proves that appendIssue against a file with no trailing newline produces a well-formed JSONL file (two records on two lines, not concatenated on one).
    4. `sd sync` (success, no-op, and git-failure paths) emits the standard '✓ ...' brand line on stdout (or printError on stderr) and, when --json is set, emits the canonical {success, command, ...} payload — verified by tests in src/commands/sync.test.ts.
    5. All 'Invalid --X value: <val>. Valid: …' error messages in src/commands/ and src/format.ts use the same delimiter (`|`), verified by grep + a focused test.
  behavior_invariant
    Every user-observable behavior of the `sd` CLI stays identical: identical exit codes, identical JSON payload shapes and key order for every command, identical human-readable text (modulo the consolidated `Valid: a|b|c` wording for invalid-enum errors, which is itself a stated normalization), identical lock semantics in `src/store.ts`, identical `.seeds/issues.jsonl|templates.jsonl|plans.jsonl` on-disk format. `bun test` must stay at 953 pass / 0 fail (plus any new regression tests added for the appender bug), `bun run lint` / `bun run typecheck` / `bun run check:size` / `bun run check:debt` must stay green, and `bunx knip` must report zero unused exports and zero configuration hints when the patrol lands.

  seeds-3918  [closed]  Clean stale knip.json entries (pino-pretty, bun-types)

  seeds-b9fa  [open]  Route sd sync output through printSuccess + outputJson error path
  seeds-1b6b  [open]  Fix appendIssue/appendTemplate/appendPlan trailing-newline edge case in src/store.ts

content.thinking:
I see the issue now—when `git status` runs against a non-repo directory, it returns exit code 128 with empty stdout, so the `changed` variable stays empty and the no-op path executes without throwing an error. I need to find a different way to trigger the failure case.

The safest approach is to initialize a fresh git repo but skip the user configuration, then write changes to the `.seeds/` directory and run sync. This way, when `git commit` tries to execute, it'll fail because git requires user.email and user.name to be set.
